Mala del Brenta.description: Italian criminal organization based in Veneto, founded in the late 1970s by Felice Maniero. It engaged in kidnappings, robberies, drug and arms trafficking, money laundering, and murders. The group was heavily hit in the 1990s after Maniero's arrest and collaboration with authorities.

descriptions: Mala del Brenta facilitated collaboration between southern mafias, including Camorra, and local crime.
edges.evidence_quote: favorirono la collaborazione tra le mafie meridionali e la piccola criminalità locale

descriptions: Mala del Brenta had business relationships with various Camorra families.
edges.evidence_quote: venivano accertati frequenti “rapporti d'affari” con esponenti della Camorra , appartenenti alla famiglia Guida, più recentemente a quella dei Giuliano , al Clan Fidanzati e alla Famiglia Ciulla

descriptions: Maniero made an agreement with a Casalesi member to split gambling proceeds in Modena.
edges.evidence_quote: Da segnalare è anche un accordo con Giuseppe Caterino, detto Peppinotto, appartenente al Clan dei Casalesi... Maniero e Caterino si accordarono per dividersi a metà i proventi del gioco d'azzardo nella città emiliana.

descriptions: Mala del Brenta had business relationships with the Fidanzati mafia group in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: Oltre ai legami con il gruppo mafioso facente capo ai Fidanzati di Milano e a Salvatore Enea, venivano accertati frequenti “rapporti d'affari” con esponenti della Camorra...

descriptions: Maniero allied with the Maritan brothers' gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Inoltre Maniero stringe alleanze con altre bande criminali del Veneto, come quella dei fratelli Maritan a San Donà di Piave o dei fratelli Rizzi a Venezia.

descriptions: The Rizzi brothers attempted to break away from Maniero's organization, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: La tentata scissione da parte dell'organizzazione veneziana è uno dei fatti più noti... I Rizzi, che rispondevano comunque a Felice Maniero, non volendo più sottostare all'autorità di Faccia d'angelo, decisero di eliminare Giancarlo Millo...

descriptions: Maniero initially allied with the Rizzi brothers, but later they attempted a split and were killed.
edges.evidence_quote: Inoltre Maniero stringe alleanze con altre bande criminali del Veneto, come quella dei fratelli Maritan a San Donà di Piave o dei fratelli Rizzi a Venezia.

descriptions: Mestrini group had strong ties to the Mala del Brenta.
edges.evidence_quote: The "Mestrini" group, based in Mestre, on the immediate Venetian mainland: a powerful group with strong ties to the Mala del Brenta

descriptions: San Donà di Piave cartel was involved with Mala del Brenta; Maritan became capo under Maniero after rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The San Donà di Piave cartel, under the leadership of Silvano Maritan, nicknamed "The President", protégé of the confined Cosa Nostra boss Salvatore Contorno, the rivalry with another cartel based in the same area caused a number of violent confrontations and subsequently placed Maritan as capo of the eastern part of the province of Venice, that is until he gave up a percentage of his assets to Maniero.

descriptions: Veneziani were later incorporated under the central authority of the Mala del Brenta.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1980s, just before the emergence of the Mala del Brenta, there were already a few organized crime syndicates that would later be incorporated under the central authority of the Mala del Brenta. These organizations included: ... - The "Veneziani"

descriptions: Nuova Mala del Brenta is the successor of the original Mala del Brenta.
edges.evidence_quote: The group is believed to have emerged in the late 1990s as a successor to the original Mala del Brenta

descriptions: SSA and CPB were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In northern Shan State, the SSA came into conflict with the Communist Party of Burma (CPB)

descriptions: UWSA fights against Shan State Army (RCSS).
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups, such as the Shan State Army (RCSS)

descriptions: UWSA allied with Tatmadaw to fight against Shan State Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups, such as the Shan State Army (RCSS).

descriptions: Torcasio is allied with Gualtieri.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Torcasio è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Lamezia Terme alleata dei Gualtieri

descriptions: Gualtieri and Cerra are allied with Torcasio and together fight Iannazzo-Giampà.
edges.evidence_quote: operanti a Capizzaglie di Nicastro . Storia Anni '70 Anni '80 Anni '90 Anni 2000 - Faida di Lamezia Terme Nel 2000 scoppia una faida ( faida di Lamezia Terme ) che si trascina ancora oggi tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Baby Regulators is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

descriptions: Mannolo and Zoffreo are involved together in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 dicembre 2019 si conclude l'operazione Infectio che porta a 27 provvedimenti contro affiliati dei Trapasso, Mannolo e Zoffreo accusati a vario titolo di traffico di droga

Jacks of Hearts Club.description: A criminal organization that operated in the Russian Empire from 1871 to 1875, composed largely of Russian nobility and wealthy merchants. The group specialized in fraud, forgery, and deception, and was involved in at least one murder. Their trial was unprecedented due to the high social status of the defendants.

descriptions: Jamaat al-Dawah ila al-Quran wal-Sunnah is a subgroup and ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Subgroups ... Jamaat al-Dawah ila al-Quran wal-Sunnah (since 2010)

descriptions: Morabito supports Sicilian families Fiandaca, Morso, Rinzivillo, Emmanuello via Albanian and Bosnian criminals.
edges.evidence_quote: spalleggiatori delle famiglie siciliane Fiandaca, Morso, Rinzivillo e Emmanuello di Gela

'Ndrina Ferraro.description: The Ferraro are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina, Italy. They are allied with the Raccosta family and part of the local 'ndrina of Oppido Mamertina. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud with the Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo clans, resulting in over 30 deaths. In 2016, boss Giuseppe Ferraro was arrested after 18 years as a fugitive.

descriptions: Bonarrigo is in feud with Ferraro.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti- Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Bonarrigo is part of the alliance in faida with Ferraro.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti -Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Ferraro (as part of Ferraro-Raccosta) is in conflict with Bonarrigo.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1992 scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Oppido Mamertina tra i Ferraro-Raccosta e i Mazzagatti - Polimeni-Bonarrigo

descriptions: Mammoliti-Rugolo supported Ferraro-Raccosta during the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2003, ormai in carcere, Saverio Mammoliti si dissocia dall'organizzazione e confessa che durante la faida i Mammoliti dettero l'appoggio ai Ferraro-Raccosta

descriptions: Ferraro (as part of Ferraro-Raccosta) is in conflict with Mazzagatti.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1992 scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Oppido Mamertina tra i Ferraro-Raccosta e i Mazzagatti - Polimeni-Bonarrigo

descriptions: Ferraro (as part of Ferraro-Raccosta) is in conflict with Polimeni.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1992 scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Oppido Mamertina tra i Ferraro-Raccosta e i Mazzagatti - Polimeni-Bonarrigo

descriptions: Ferraro and Raccosta together are in conflict with Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1992 scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Oppido Mamertina tra i Ferraro-Raccosta e i Mazzagatti - Polimeni-Bonarrigo

descriptions: Ferraro and Raccosta are allied.
edges.evidence_quote: I Ferraro sono una 'ndrina di Oppido Mamertina alleata dei Raccosta dello stesso paese

descriptions: Ferraro and Raccosta are allied together in a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti- Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Ferraro and Raccosta are allied against the Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti -Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Mammoliti sided with Ferraro-Raccosta in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2011 sembra riesplosa la faida tra le due consorterie e questa volta sarebbero stati coinvolti anche i Mammoliti a favore di questi ultimi.

descriptions: Mammoliti sided with Ferraro-Raccosta in the renewed conflict from 2011.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2011, la battaglia tra le due consorterie sembra riesplosa, e questa volta sarebbero stati coinvolti anche i Mammoliti a favore di questi ultimi.

descriptions: Mazzagatti is in feud with Ferraro.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti- Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Mazzagatti alliance is in faida (feud) with Ferraro.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti -Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Polimeni is in feud with Ferraro.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti- Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Polimeni is part of the alliance in faida with Ferraro.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1992 al 1998 i Mazzagatti -Polimeni-Bonarrigo sono in faida con i Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Vangelo is a higher rank within the Società maggiore, above Santa.
edges.evidence_quote: un grado superiore il Vangelo

descriptions: Blue Angels and Mad Dogs were in a feud after a former Blue Angels member formed the Mad Dogs.
edges.evidence_quote: A feud developed between the Blue Angels and the Mad Dogs Motorcycle Club after senior Blue Angels member Daniel Laponder broke away and formed the Mad Dogs in 2023.

descriptions: Matsuda-gumi attempted to assassinate Taoka, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Taoka survived a 1978 assassination attempt when he was shot in the back of the neck by a member of the Matsuda-gumi (松田組), a rival gang to the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi fought Matsuda-gumi in the Osaka War.
edges.evidence_quote: Osaka War (1975–1978, against the Matsuda-gumi)

descriptions: ISIS–K formed an alliance with Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin.
edges.evidence_quote: the newly ordained ISIS–K forged significant alliances with other Sunni jihadist movements in the region to include the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (IMU), Islamic Movement of Turkmenistan (IMT), Uyghur East Turkestan Islamic Party (ETIP), Jamaat Ansarullah, Harakat Islami Tajikistan, and the Islamic Jihad Renaissance Party of Tajikistan (IJRPT), Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin, and Balochi militant groups including the Pakistani Jundullah movement and Iranian Jundullah movement

descriptions: Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin is a non-state ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state allies ... Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin (denied from 2016–2021, openly since 2021)

descriptions: Granatiero family accused of being in contact with Romito clan.
edges.evidence_quote: la famiglia Granatiero, titolare di alcuni beni sequestrati, è accusata di essere in contatto con i Romito.

Shiners.description: Gangs of Irish immigrants active in Bytown (later Ottawa) during the 1830s. Formed after the Rideau Canal's completion left many Irish unemployed, they used intimidation and violence to create jobs, sparking the Shiners' War. Engaged in street fights, robbery, and murder until armed patrols suppressed them in 1837.

descriptions: The two groups were in intense confrontation, fighting for control of fuel supply routes.
edges.evidence_quote: La intensa confrontación entre la NDPVF y NDV parece haber sido provocada por política de enfrentamiento de Asiri y el financiamiento del NDPVF a la campaña de Peter Odili, gobernador del estado de Rivers tras las elecciones de abril de 2003.

descriptions: NDV was protected by government forces from NDPVF attacks, indicating conflict between NDPVF and NDV.
edges.evidence_quote: El gobierno del estado respondió la escalada de la campaña en su contra mediante el envío de la policía, el ejército y la marina que ocupó Port Harcourt, en junio de 2004. Las fuerzas del gobierno colaboró con el NDV durante el verano, y dieron protección a milicianos del NDV de los ataques de la NDPVF.

descriptions: NDV was used as a paramilitary group to eliminate NDPVF, confirming conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Odili retiró su apoyo financiero al NDPVF y comenzó a apoyar NDV de Tom, usándolo como grupo paramilitar para acabar con el NDPVF.

Genovese crime family.description: The Genovese crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City and New Jersey. Founded by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano in the 1930s, it is the oldest and largest of the Five Families. Known for its secrecy and white-collar rackets, it has been considered the most powerful Mafia family in the United States. The family is currently led by Liborio 'Barney' Bellomo.

descriptions: The 116th Street Crew is a faction (sub-unit) of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The 116th Street Crew , also known as the Uptown Crew , is a faction of the Genovese crime family .

descriptions: The Genovese family is a constituent family of the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: la familia criminal Genovese

descriptions: Genovese family holds a seat on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Genovese (1931–present)

descriptions: The AZ Syndicate had ties to the Genovese crime family dating back to Prohibition.
edges.evidence_quote: Their ties to the Chicago Outfit and New York City 's Genovese crime family date back to the Prohibition period.

descriptions: Bonanno acting capo Pisciotti assaulted Genovese associates, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Pisciotti assaulted several Genovese crime family associates in a Little Italy restaurant.

descriptions: Bonanno family is allied with Genovese crime family per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Genovese crime family

descriptions: Genovese family sought consensus to kill Galante, involving Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: Genovese crime family boss Frank Tieri began contacting Cosa Nostra leaders to build a consensus for Galante's murder

descriptions: Bonanno and Genovese families jointly operated an illegal gambling establishment.
edges.evidence_quote: The indictment alleged the Bonannos and Genoveses operated a joint illegal gambling establishment in Lynbrook called the Gran Caffe.

descriptions: A Bonanno capo and a Genovese soldier jointly ran an illegal gambling operation.
edges.evidence_quote: Frascone was involved in a large-scale illegal gambling operation along with Genovese soldier Victor Colletti.

descriptions: Members of both families were arrested together in a racketeering case, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Members from the Bonnano as well as the Genovese families were arrested.

descriptions: Members of the Genovese family attempted to kill a Bufalino associate who tried to become independent.
edges.evidence_quote: Muruca was lured to a barn in Agawam, Massachusetts on August 11, 1981, where he was shot five times by members of the Springfield faction of the Genovese family, on the orders of Anthony Guarnieri.

descriptions: Bufalino family maintained a close alliance with Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained a close alliance with the Genovese crime family due to common interests in New York City and northern New Jersey

descriptions: The Genovese family approved the promotion of Domenico Violi to underboss of the Buffalo family.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2017, he promoted Canadian mobster Domenico Violi to Underboss of the family with the approval of the New York Bonanno , Genovese and Colombo families.

descriptions: The Charlestown Gang performed contract killings for the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The McLaughlin brothers forged ties with the Mafia, carrying out contract killings for the Patriarca crime family in Boston and the Genovese crime family in New York City.

descriptions: A Cava affiliate had ties with the Genovese family boss Adolfo Bruno.
edges.evidence_quote: Michele Palmese ... aveva tessuto rapporti con Adolfo Bruno - boss della famiglia Genovese - e i figli di questo.

descriptions: Cleveland family developed ties with the Genovese crime family under Scalish.
edges.evidence_quote: the Cleveland family developed ties with ... the Genovese crime family in New York.

descriptions: Scarpa, a Colombo member, killed a Genovese mobster, indicating conflict between the families.
edges.evidence_quote: Over the next few weeks, Scarpa and his associates killed Genovese mobster Thomas Amato and Orena loyalists Rosario Nastasa, Vincent Fusaro and James Malpiso.

descriptions: The Colombo and Genovese families were feuding, but the account of the Gallo murder reduced tensions between them.
edges.evidence_quote: Umbertos was owned by associates of the Genovese crime family, which would normally imply the Genoveses had given their blessing to a killing on their territory, but Luparelli's account, that the shooting was a spontaneous unplanned act without approval from high-ranking mafiosi, took pressure off the feuding Colombo and Genovese families.

descriptions: A Genovese soldier participated in the murder of Joe Gallo, acting with Colombo associates.
edges.evidence_quote: Genovese soldier Carmine "Sonny Pinto" DiBiase

descriptions: Spado was an associate of the Colombo family and also connected to Pyle, who had Genovese ties, suggesting cooperation between Colombo and Genovese.
edges.evidence_quote: Ori Spado, Hollywood fixer and gangster, and associate of Colombo family underboss Sonny Franzese

descriptions: Gallo crew was exiled to the Genovese family, indicating a transfer of members.
edges.evidence_quote: Colombo supporters led by Carmine Persico eventually won the second war after the exiling of the remaining Gallo crew to the Genovese family in 1975.

descriptions: A Genovese family associate provided evidence against a Colombo member, indicating interaction but not necessarily cooperation or conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2002, DeRoss was convicted of extortion, after Genovese family associate turncoat Michael D'Urso taped DeRoss confessing to conspiracy of obtaining 'protection money' from two deli supply companies

descriptions: A Colombo associate transferred to become a soldier in the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Gerard Pappa — a family associate who transferred to become a soldier in the Genovese crime family

descriptions: The Dallas crime family was allied with the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chicago Outfit , Genovese crime family , Kansas City crime family , New Orleans crime family , Trafficante crime family

descriptions: Genovese is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Genovese crime family

descriptions: DeCavalcante family was previously affiliated with the Genovese family before being taken over by Gambino.
edges.evidence_quote: After Rotondo's killing, the Gambino family took control of the DeCavalcante family, which had previously been affiliated with the Genovese family.

descriptions: The Purple Gang developed its closest ties with the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: It developed its 'closest ties' with the Genovese family

descriptions: The Gallo crew joined the Genovese family after leaving the Colombo family.
edges.evidence_quote: Albert Gallo and his remaining crew left the Colombo family and peacefully joined the Genovese family.

descriptions: After a 1974 agreement, Albert Gallo and his remaining crew joined the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Albert and his remaining crew to join the Genovese family.

descriptions: Gambino conspired to frame Genovese.
edges.evidence_quote: Gambino and Luciano then allegedly provided a part of the $100,000 paid to a Puerto Rican drug dealer to falsely implicate Genovese in a drug deal.

descriptions: Genovese boss Gigante conspired to kill Gotti, making the families enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Gotti's strongest enemy was Genovese crime family boss Vincent 'Chin' Gigante, a former Castellano ally.

descriptions: Gambino conspired with Lucchese to remove Genovese, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: After assuming power, Gambino started conspiring with Lucchese to remove their former ally Genovese.

descriptions: The Luciano family supported Anastasia's ascension.
edges.evidence_quote: With the support of Frank Costello, boss of the Luciano crime family, the Commission confirmed Anastasia's ascension as boss of what was now the Anastasia crime family.

descriptions: The Gambino family lists Genovese as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Detroit Partnership, Genovese crime family, Inzerillo Mafia clan, ...

descriptions: Gambino and Genovese members were indicted together in a kickback scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: Gambino family capo Frank 'Calypso' Camuso, soldier Louis Astuto and associate Robert 'Rusty' Baselice were indicted along 20 other defendants including Genovese family soldier Christopher 'Jerry' Chierchio.

descriptions: Gambino, Genovese, and Bonanno members jointly controlled poker games.
edges.evidence_quote: he was arrested for getting money from two crooked poker games regularly held in New York City that were controlled by Gambino, Genovese and Bonanno members.

descriptions: Gambino soldier Joseph Francolino and Genovese capo Alphonse Malangone were convicted together for controlling carting industry.
edges.evidence_quote: Francolino was found guilty of alleged mob control of the private carting industry in New York, alongside Genovese family capo Alphonse Malangone.

descriptions: A Gambino capo worked with a Genovese capo on construction projects, indicating cooperation between the families.
edges.evidence_quote: It is noted he was involved with Genovese capo John Ardito contracting and subcontracting projects in the Bronx and Westchester area.

descriptions: Gambino family cooperated with Genovese family in New Haven County.
edges.evidence_quote: The family has also cooperated with the Genovese family in New Haven County.

descriptions: Gambino soldier Fappiano engaged in extortion with the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Fappiano admitted to corruption, bribery and the extortion of construction companies based in Manhattan and Brooklyn, with the Genovese crime family.

descriptions: Gambino and Genovese were initially allied in a plot to take over the Commission, but later Gambino conspired to remove Genovese.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Lucchese-Genovese alliance (1953–1985) between Carlo Gambino, Tommy Lucchese, and Vito Genovese began with a plot to take over the Mafia Commission by murdering family bosses Frank Costello and Albert Anastasia.

descriptions: The 116th Street Crew is a crew operating under the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: 116th Street Crew operates in Upper Manhattan and The Bronx.

descriptions: Genovese family originated from the Morello gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese crime family originated from the Morello gang of East Harlem, the first Mafia family in New York City.

descriptions: Genovese is part of the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime activities in New York City and New Jersey as part of the American Mafia.

descriptions: Maranzano plotted to kill Luciano.
edges.evidence_quote: By September 1931, Maranzano realized Luciano was a threat, and hired Vincent "Mad Dog" Coll, an Irish gangster, to kill him.

descriptions: Luciano initially allied with Maranzano to betray Masseria.
edges.evidence_quote: In a secret deal with Maranzano, Luciano agreed to engineer Masseria's death in return for taking over his rackets and becoming Maranzano's second-in-command.

descriptions: Genovese family lists Bonanno as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Bufalino crime family, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Dallas crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Trafficante crime family, Hells Angels MC, Latin Kings, Pagan's MC, Purple Gang

descriptions: Genovese and Bonanno members were charged together in a conspiracy.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 4, 2016, the United States Attorney of New York charged 46 Mafia leaders with racketeering conspiracy... The 46 defendants were leaders, members, and associates of the Genovese, Gambino, Luchese, Bonanno, and Philadelphia crime families.

descriptions: Genovese and Bonanno families jointly operated illegal gambling operations.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family and the Bonanno family jointly operated several illegal gambling operations while using front businesses in Queens and Long Island to launder illegal profits.

descriptions: Genovese soldier Victor Colletti and Bonanno soldier Anthony Frascone led an illegal gambling operation together.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2005, Colletti was indicted for illegal gambling, along with 16 other defendants associated with the Genovese and Bonanno families.

descriptions: Members of both families were indicted together for racketeering and other charges, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 16, 2022, Polito was indicted along with Genovese family soldier Joseph Macario, Genovese family associates Salvatore Rubino and Joseph Rutigliano, Bonnano family capo Anthony "Little Anthony" Pipitone, Bonanno family soldier Vito Pipitone, Bonanno family associate Agostino Gabriele and Nassau County Police Detective Hector Rosario.

descriptions: Genovese soldier Joseph Macario ran an illegal gambling operation in association with the Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: since at least May 2012, he was the head of a lucrative illegal gambling operation in Lynbrook, New York in association with the Bonanno family, with capo Anthony "Little Anthony" Pipitone on behalf of the Bonanno family.

descriptions: Zoccolillo was an associate of both Genovese and Bonanno families, indicating a link between the families.
edges.evidence_quote: Anthony Zoccolillo — former associate of the Genovese and Bonanno families

descriptions: Genovese family lists Bonanno as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Detroit, Gambino, Lucchese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia, K&A Gang

descriptions: Genovese soldier Tenaglia was previously affiliated with Bonanno family before transferring.
edges.evidence_quote: Tenaglia was initially affiliated with the Brooklyn faction of the Bonanno family, the Motion Lounge crew, which was formerly headed by Dominick "Sonny Black" Napolitano, and was transferred to the Genovese family.

descriptions: Genovese family lists Bufalino as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Bufalino crime family, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Dallas crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Trafficante crime family, Hells Angels MC, Latin Kings, Pagan's MC, Purple Gang

descriptions: Genovese has ties with Buffalo crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.

descriptions: Genovese family has ties with Buffalo crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the ... Buffalo crime families.

descriptions: Genovese family has ties with Buffalo family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.

descriptions: Anthony Salerno of the Genovese family reached out to a Buffalo Crime Family associate for a hit.
edges.evidence_quote: Salerno then reached out to Buffalo Crime Family associate and freelance hitman, Joseph Sullivan

descriptions: Buffalo family was represented by the Genovese family on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Buffalo crime family – held a seat from 1931 to 1974

descriptions: Genovese family lists Chicago Outfit as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Bufalino crime family, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Dallas crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Trafficante crime family, Hells Angels MC, Latin Kings, Pagan's MC, Purple Gang

descriptions: The Chicago Outfit is often represented by the Genovese family on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Chicago Outfit (1931–present), often represented by the Genovese family

descriptions: Genovese has ties with Cleveland crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.

descriptions: Genovese family has ties with Cleveland crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the ... Cleveland ... crime families.

descriptions: Genovese family has ties with Cleveland family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.

descriptions: Cleveland family was represented by the Genovese family on the Commission.

descriptions: Genovese and Gambino members were jointly indicted and sentenced in the same operation.

descriptions: Genovese supported Scarfo in Philadelphia war, gaining Atlantic City access.
edges.evidence_quote: After the 1980 murder of Philadelphia boss Angelo "Gentle Don" Bruno, Gigante and Lombardo began manipulating the rival factions in the war-torn Philadelphia family. They finally gave their support to Philadelphia mobster Nicodemo "Little Nicky" Scarfo, who in return gave the Genovese mobsters permission to operate in Atlantic City in 1982.

descriptions: Lucchese and Genovese families cooperated to target John Gotti.

descriptions: Lucchese allied with Genovese and Gambino to take over the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: That same year, Lucchese formed an alliance with Luciano crime family underboss Vito Genovese and Anastasia crime family underboss Carlo Gambino with the long-term goal of gaining control of the Commission.

descriptions: The Morello family was the predecessor of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: They were the predecessors of what eventually became known as the Genovese crime family .

descriptions: Carlos Marcello began working for Frank Costello of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: This ordeal got him involved with Frank Costello, leader of the Genovese crime family in New York City, where he began working for Costello.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Genovese crime family as an ally.

descriptions: Members of New Orleans and Genovese families were arrested together for video poker infiltration.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 1994, following an FBI sting dubbed "Operation Hard Crust", Carollo with 16 members of the Marcello, Gambino and Genovese families were arrested on charges of infiltrating the newly legalized Louisiana video poker industry.

descriptions: Phillip Kastel was an associate of both the New Orleans crime family (Marcello) and the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Kastel, a New York-born Jewish mobster, was an associate of the Marcello and Genovese families.

descriptions: Patriarca formed strong relationships with Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: During his reign as boss, Patriarca formed strong relationships with the New York-based Genovese and Colombo crime families

descriptions: DeMarco is suspected of linking the Genovese crew and Patriarca family, suggesting cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Christian 'Chris' DeMarco ... with alleged ties to the Springfield MA Genovese Crew. As of 2026 it is alleged that DeMarco acts as a 'link' between the Springfield MA Genovese Crew and Patriarca Family

descriptions: The Genovese family claimed territory and was involved in the dispute leading to Bruno's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York 's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.

descriptions: Genovese family had territory claims against Philadelphia family, leading to Bruno's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: A complex dispute involving disgruntled subordinates and territory claims by New York's Genovese crime family led to Bruno's murder in 1980.

descriptions: The Genovese family's territory claims contributed to the murder of Philadelphia boss Angelo Bruno, indicating conflict.

descriptions: Genovese boss Gigante ordered murder contracts against Philadelphia family members as retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: He testified against Genovese crime family boss Vincent Gigante in 1997 and claimed Gigante ordered 6 murder contracts as retaliation for murdering Philadelphia bosses Angelo Bruno and Phil Testa without approval from the other crime families, specifically in New York.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox, despite earlier conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Genovese crime family

descriptions: The Pittsburgh family formed a strong alliance with the Genovese family, who represented them at Commission meetings.
edges.evidence_quote: Amato began expanding his influence over the gambling rackets in and around Allegheny County, and formed a strong alliance with the Genovese crime family in New York City, who represented Pittsburgh at meetings of the Commission.

descriptions: Mogilevich's associate met with Genovese crime family members to arrange a toxic waste dumping scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1998, an FBI informant told the bureau that one of Mogilevich's chief lieutenants in Los Angeles met two Russians from New York City with Genovese crime family ties to broker a scheme to dump American toxic waste in Russia or Ukraine.

descriptions: A Genovese crime family hitman shot a Tanglewood Boys member during an argument.
edges.evidence_quote: Zanfardino pulled out a gun and shot Mazzarelli and pointed the gun at Santorelli before leaving the scene.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.

descriptions: Trafficante family's Miami faction leader Raffa cooperated with Genovese family member Mamone in a money laundering operation.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1990s, Raffa worked with Genovese family's New Jersey faction mobster John Mamone, who began living in Pompano Beach.

descriptions: The Genovese crime family sought control over the convention center, threatening the Westies.
edges.evidence_quote: In the Irish/Italian Mob War of the 1970s, the Irish mob saw an increased threat from the Italian Mafia as the Genovese crime family sought control over the soon-to-be-built Jacob K. Javits Convention Center.

descriptions: The Genovese crime family sought control over the convention center, leading to conflict with the Westies.
edges.evidence_quote: the Irish mob saw an increased threat from the Italian Mafia as the Genovese crime family sought control over the soon-to-be-built Jacob K. Javits Convention Center

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang partnered with Genovese crime family in horse race-fixing.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang partnered with mobsters from the Genovese, Bruno and Zerilli crime families in infiltrating race courses

Compton Executioners.description: The Compton Executioners is a deputy gang within the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department (LASD), based at the Compton station. Founded by former 2000 Boys member Andy Toone, its members share a distinctive calf tattoo. The gang is involved in police corruption and has been linked to the Lynwood Vikings. In July 2021, Congresswoman Maxine Waters called for a Department of Justice inquiry into the gang.

descriptions: The founder of the Compton Executioners was a former member of the 2000 Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was founded by former 2000 Boys member Andy Toone.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Lynwood Vikings as allies of the Compton Executioners.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lynwood Vikings

descriptions: The infobox lists Compton Executioners as an ally of the Lynwood Vikings.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Compton Executioners

descriptions: Black Jackets and Red Legions Stuttgart are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: United Tribuns , Red Legions Stuttgart , AK81 , X-Team

descriptions: The Lago clan fought against the allied Contino and Marfella clans from 1991 to 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: prima faida di Pianura : svoltasi tra il 1991 e il 2000 tra il clan Lago e i clan Contino e Marfella, questi ultimi due alleati

descriptions: Indian Posse members killed a member of the rival MOB Squad.
edges.evidence_quote: beating to death Paris Bruce, a member of the rival MOB Squad

descriptions: MOB Squad is an affiliated organization of Manitoba Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: Saskatchewan Warriors, Alberta Warriors, CENTRAL, Loyalty Honour Silence and MOB Squad are some of their more well known affiliations.

The Company.description: Organized crime syndicate based in Hawaii, founded in 1969 by Wilford Pulawa. Controlled criminal activities in Hawaii from the late 1960s to the mid-1990s, involved in illegal gambling, prostitution, racketeering, extortion, and drug trafficking. Extended influence to mainland US states including California and Nevada.

descriptions: The Company had both connections and rivalry with the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The syndicate made connections with figures in the American Mafia, but in some cases also had a rivalry with some of them.

descriptions: Los Fatales is a subgroup of Los Choneros.
edges.evidence_quote: Esta organización criminal y delincuencial ... está conformada por dos peligrosas ramificaciones delictivas llamadas ' Los Fatales '

descriptions: Conflict between Bandidos and DBD member led to retaliation killing.
edges.evidence_quote: The attack was carried out in retaliation after a fight broke out between a Death Before Dishonour (DBD, a Rebels-affiliated club) member and a Bandidos prospect earlier that day

descriptions: JI originated from Darul Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: JI has its roots in Darul Islam (DI, meaning "House of Islam"), a radical Islamist/anti-colonialist movement in Indonesia in the 1940s.

descriptions: Vieux-Doo Dawgs took over the Midwest Drifters, indicating a structural takeover.
edges.evidence_quote: Once established it took over another club, the Midwest Drifters, and uses them to run errands and provide cash.

descriptions: Ricci-Sarno group is hostile to Marco Mariano and his followers, sending violent messages to discourage reconquest.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli episodi di violenza sarebbero messaggi nei confronti di Marco Mariano e dei suoi fedeli allo scopo di scoraggiare qualsiasi velleità di riconquista dei Quartieri Spagnoli, dove i Ricci-Sarno gestiscono numerose piazze di spaccio, gestite sempre dagli stessi spacciatori, i quali pagano una percentuale al boss di turno.

descriptions: Ricci group is allied with Sarno clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Nei Quartieri Spagnoli, in seguito alla caduta dei clan Di Biasi e Russo, è emerso il gruppo dei Ricci, facente capo al pregiudicato Enrico Ricci, ex commerciante, ed ai suoi due figli, alleati dei Sarno di Ponticelli.

descriptions: Clan Sarno allied with the Ricci clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Le alleanze dei Sarno compresero il clan Ricci sui Quartieri Spagnoli

descriptions: The Bellocco and Cimato are grouped together as a single cosca in the operation.
edges.evidence_quote: operazione Eclissi... contro appartenenti alle cosche di 'ndrangheta "Bellocco-Cimato"

descriptions: Sons of Silence had a power struggle with Dead Men in 1972.
edges.evidence_quote: the brawl involved a total of twenty-two participants and was the result of a power struggle between the Sons of Silence and two other motorcycle clubs, the Dead Men and a group known as Philtown.
